records.ety: [OE. thicke, AS. þicce; akin to D. dik, OS. thikki, OHG. dicchi thick, dense, G. dick thick, Icel. þykkr, þjökkr, and probably to Gael. & Ir. tiugh. Cf. Tight.]

defs: Measuring in the third dimension other than length and breadth, or in general dimension other than length; -- said of a solid body; as, a timber seven inches thick.
quotes: Were it as thick as is a branched oak.
My little finger shall be thicker than my father's loins.

defs: Having more depth or extent from one surface to its opposite than usual; not thin or slender; as, a thick plank; thick cloth; thick paper; thick neck.
Dense; not thin; inspissated; as, thick vapors. Also used figuratively; as, thick darkness.
quotes: Make the gruel thick and slab.

defs: Not transparent or clear; hence, turbid, muddy, or misty; as, the water of a river is apt to be thick after a rain.
Abundant, close, or crowded in space; closely set; following in quick succession; frequently recurring.
quotes: The people were gathered thick together.
Black was the forest; thick with beech it stood.

defs: Not having due distinction of syllables, or good articulation; indistinct; as, a thick utterance.
Dull; not quick; as, thick of fearing.
quotes: His dimensions to any thick sight were invincible.
We have been thick ever since.
notes: ☞ Thick is often used in the formation of compounds, most of which are self-explaining; as, thick-barred, thick-bodied, thick-coming, thick-cut, thick-flying, thick-growing, thick-leaved, thick-lipped, thick-necked, thick-planted, thick-ribbed, thick-shelled, thick-woven, and the like.

defs: The thickest part, or the time when anything is thickest.
quotes: In the thick of the dust and smoke.
Through the thick they heard one rudely rush.
He through a little window cast his sight Through thick of bars, that gave a scanty light.
Through thick and thin she followed him.
He became the panegyrist, through thick and thin, of a military frenzy.

defs: Closely; as, a plat of ground thick sown.
To a great depth, or to a greater depth than usual; as, land covered thick with manure.

quotes: The nightmare Life-in-death was she, Who thicks man's blood with cold.
